title: FOSTER YOUTH TUITION & FEE WAIVER - 211info
text: FEDERAL STUDENT AID FOSTER YOUTH TUITION & FEE WAIVER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Tuition and fee waiver available to eligible foster youth students for the equivalent of 4 years of undergraduate study 400 Maryland Avenue SW Washington, DC 20202 Eligibility: Former or current foster youth: -Must have been in ODHS or a federally recognized Oregon Tribe's care for at least 180 days after age 14 -Must not have left care before their 16th birthday Adopted former foster youth: -Must have spent at least 180 days in substitute care -Must have been adopted on or after January 1, 2012 Youth who experienced homelessness: -Must have experienced homelessness for at least 180 days between the ages of 14 and 24 -Must have received services from an ODHS-contracted organization or a host home while homeless -Must have been at least 16 years old when last receiving services Hours: Application deadline: October 1-June 30 of the award year, or the student’s last day of class for the academic year (whichever is earliest) Email (800) 433-3243 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (800) 433-3243 Federal Student Aid Information Center Other (503) 945-6612 DHS Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Eligible applicants must submit a completed FAFSA by visiting website FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Completed FAFSA AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Student Financial Aid * Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues Student Financial Aid * Former Foster Children Other Services Offered At This Location: EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - FREE APPLICATION FOR FEDERAL STUDENT AID (FAFSA) If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. FEDERAL STUDENT AID
